title: IP Address Lookup: Geolocation, ISP & Network Info
description: Your IP address reveals more than just your location. See what ISPs and websites can track about your network, including your exact coordinates and ASN.

acceptedAnswer.text: Your IP address is a unique identifier assigned to your device on the internet. You can see your current public IP address at the top of this page, which is detected automatically when you visit.

IP lookup works by querying databases that map IP address blocks to geographical locations, ISPs, and organizations. When you enter an IP, we query these databases to find the registered location and network details.

IP address geolocation is the process of estimating the real-world geographic location of an internet-connected device. This typically includes the country, region, city, and approximate latitude/longitude coordinates.

Public IP info generally includes your ISP (Internet Service Provider), ASN (Autonomous System Number), organization, country, state/region, and city. It does not include your exact street address or personal identity.

IP geolocation accuracy varies. It is typically 95-99% accurate at the country level, 55-80% accurate for region/state, and 50-75% for city. The exact coordinates provided are usually the center of the city or postal code, not a specific house.
